source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BROCCOLI

Broccoli AI is building the future of customer service for the trades. Our AI voice agents help plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses deliver fast, reliable, and empathetic support around the clock. Our mission is simple: to democratize great customer service so every contractor, no matter their size, can give customers the experience they deserve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Implementation Manager at Broccoli, you’ll own the onboarding experience for new customers—from setup to success. You’ll be the bridge between our AI agents and real-world HVAC, plumbing, and electrical businesses, making sure every contractor goes live smoothly, sees results fast, and gets real value from day one.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role where you’ll work closely with customers, product, and engineering to bring AI into the workflows of some of the fastest-growing service businesses in the country.




YOUR CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead onboarding for new customers, setting up their AI agents to handle calls, book jobs, and integrate with CRMs like ServiceTitan.

 - Customize prompt flows and voice logic to match each business’s tone, processes, and goals.

 - Train owners and office managers on best practices, troubleshooting, and ongoing optimization.

 - Monitor early adoption closely, identify risks, and ensure accounts hit key milestones in the first 30–60 days.

 - Collaborate with product and engineering to share customer feedback and improve AI behavior and workflows.
   
   


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 3+ years in onboarding, customer success, or technical implementation—ideally at a fast-paced SaaS, automation, or AI company.

 - Are a strong communicator who can make AI and technical concepts simple for non-technical users.

 - Are organized and process-driven, able to manage multiple accounts in parallel and refine onboarding playbooks.

 - Thrive in ambiguity and speed—you move fast, solve problems proactively, and help define how we scale.

Broccoli is an equal-opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practice laws.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BROCCOLI

Broccoli is building the AI operating system for home service businesses.

We work with plumbing, HVAC, and electrical contractors — the people who keep homes running — and we replace fragmented tools and manual workflows with AI agents that actually do the work.

Our AI assistants answer phones, book jobs, follow up with customers, and drive revenue — fully integrated into systems like ServiceTitan.

We started by going door-to-door, meeting 100+ contractors, and understanding how these businesses actually run. That shaped everything we’ve built. Today:

 - Hundreds of contractors use Broccoli to run their front office

 - We’ve grown from $0 → millions in ARR in under a year

 - We’re trusted by both single-location operators and the largest PE-backed roll-ups

 - Raised $25M+ from Khosla Ventures and YC
   
   


ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE, MID-MARKET @ BROCCOLI

Broccoli is building the AI intelligence layer that runs front-office operations for home service businesses — turning calls into scheduled appointments and real revenue. We’re looking for an Account Executive for Mid-Market to own a high-velocity sales motion with strong inbound demand — and the autonomy to create pipeline when needed. This role is about speed, judgment, and execution. If you like momentum, tight feedback loops, and seeing your work turn into revenue quickly, this role is for you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a high-volume sales role focused on independent operators and growing multi-location businesses. Typical deal sizes are $20K - 40K ACV, with short sales cycles and clear ROI.

Inbound will drive the majority of pipeline — but the best reps here don’t wait. You’ll prospect strategically when it makes sense to hit your number and grow accounts faster.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the full Mid-Market sales cycle — from first conversation to close.

 - Run high-quality, fast demos that clearly connect Broccoli to customer outcomes.

 - Close a high volume of deals with short, efficient sales cycles.

 - Prospect when needed — outbound to high-intent accounts, expansions, and lookalike customers.

 - Understand customer workflows quickly and tailor the pitch without over-customizing.

 - Partner closely with onboarding and customer success to ensure smooth handoffs.

 - Maintain a tight pipeline and consistently hit quota.

 - Share feedback from the field to improve messaging, pricing, and product.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 2–5+ years of experience in a high-velocity B2B SaaS sales role.

 - Proven success closing $20K–$40K ACV deals.

 - Comfort balancing inbound volume with thoughtful outbound.

 - Strong discovery, demo, and closing skills.

 - Bias toward action and ownership.

 - Clear, confident communication with operators and business owners.

Broccoli is an equal-opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practice laws.

source_jobs.title: In-House Content Creator
source_jobs.description:
About Broccoli 🥦

Our mission is to bring intelligence and reliability to real-world operations — starting with home service businesses.

We see operations as a systems problem, not a staffing problem. Answering customers, scheduling appointments, and following up consistently requires judgment, memory, and accountability — not more headcount. As LLMs make AI agents easier to build and demos easier to fake, reliability and execution become the only real differentiator. We help home service companies run their front office with AI that takes action, remembers context, and delivers outcomes you can trust.

In 2025, we scaled revenue 20× to multi-million dollars, grew the team from 5 to 20, and raised a $25M Series A led by Khosla Ventures with participation from Y Combinator.

A few things to know about us:

 - Our COO was our first customer — a plumbing company CEO — and helped shape Broccoli from day one.

 - Our customers collectively do $15B+ in annual revenue, and Broccoli sits directly in the flow of how that revenue is captured.

 - We've booked 500K+ real, verified appointments for our customers.

 - Every engineer spends time onsite with customers, listening to live calls and seeing real-world edge cases firsthand.

What You'll Do

We're looking for a Content Creator to work directly alongside our team and clients — capturing real customer stories, industry moments, behind-the-scenes footage, product launches, and daily life, and turning them into short- and long-form content people actually want to watch and share.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Create short-form content for Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, LinkedIn, and X

 - Capture behind-the-scenes footage from the team, clients, product launches, events, and meetings

 - Help founders build authentic, engaging personal brands

 - Develop and maintain recurring content formats that showcase our people, culture, and product

 - Experiment with new ideas and iterate quickly based on performance

 - Be knowledgeable on industry trends

What We're Looking For:

 - Strong videography, editing, and storytelling skills

 - Proficiency with CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools

 - Deep understanding of Reels, TikTok, Shorts, and platform-specific trends

 - Ability to take content from concept to publication independently

 - Willingness to travel

 - In-person, five days a week in San Francisco

Bonus Qualifications:

 - Experience building a personal audience or creator brand

 - Experience with founder-led or startup-focused content

 - Familiarity with AI products or the home service industry

 - Photography skills

Why Join Us?

You'll be embedded in a fast-moving company at an inflection point — with real access to founders, customers, and a product that's changing how an entire industry operates. No two weeks look the same, the team is energetic and collaborative, and there's genuine room to grow as we do. You'll travel, build real relationships, and create content that actually matters.



Broccoli is an equal-opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practice laws.

Broccoli is building the AI intelligence layer that runs operations for home service businesses — turning calls, conversations, and follow-ups into scheduled appointments and real revenue. As Broccoli’s adoption expands across PE-backed rollups and franchise groups, we’re investing in a Founding Enterprise Account Executive to build and scale our enterprise motion end-to-end. ABOUT THE ROLE

The role spans two tightly connected tracks. Success requires owning both.

Enterprise Origination & Expansion

How Broccoli enters large organizations and grows over time

 - Own enterprise relationships with PE-backed rollups and franchise groups (5+ locations).

Enterprise Deployment & Value Realization

Not a fit

ABOUT BROCCOLI AI

Broccoli AI is building the AI operating system for home service businesses.

We work with plumbing, HVAC, roofing, etc. contractors, the people who keep homes running, and replace fragmented tools and manual workflows with AI agents that actually do the work.

Our AI assistants answer phones, book jobs, follow up with customers, and drive revenue, fully integrated into systems like ServiceTitan.

We started by going door-to-door, meeting 100+ contractors, and understanding how these businesses actually run. That shaped everything we've built. Today:

 - Hundreds of contractors use Broccoli to run their front office.

 - We've grown from $0 to millions in ARR in under a year.

 - We're trusted by both single-location operators and the largest PE-backed roll-ups.

 - We've raised $25M+ from Khosla Ventures and Y Combinator.

About the role

Home services owners run real, complex businesses across HVAC, roofing, garage doors, lawn care, etc., and almost none of them can see their own operation clearly. Their CRMs bury the data. The views that would tell them what's working, where they're leaking revenue, and which lever to pull next simply don't exist. And what matters differs by vertical: the numbers that run a roofing business aren't the numbers that run a lawn care route.

You'll own that. You'll define and build the core views that let operators actually run their businesses, and you'll be the one who decides what those insights are. You'll sit with our top customers, learn their operation, and deliver the business intelligence no one else can give them.

Broccoli is growing fast, and this is a foundational role. You'll work shoulder to shoulder with Product and Engineering, and your insights will directly drive our business. If you want a seat where your analysis shapes both the customer and the company, this is it.

What you'll do

 - Sit with our customers, understand their business inside and out, how they book, dispatch, sell, and retain, and deliver the business intelligence no one else can give them.

 - Define the core views that operators run their business on, tailored to what matters in each vertical.

 - Deliver the insights, benchmarks, and reporting that move customer performance.

 - Establish the benchmarks that let an operator see where they stand, and what to do about it.

 - Partner closely with Product and Engineering throughout your role

What we're looking for

 - 6–10+ years of experience in consulting, analytics, BI, or product with a track record of turning messy operational data into insights that moved the business.

 - You can sit across from a trades business owner, earn their trust fast, and pull out what's actually going on in their operation.

 - You build for reuse and scale, seeing the patterns across customers, not just the one in front of you.

 - Bias toward building. You'd rather ship a rough answer this week than a perfect one next quarter.

 - Customer obsession. You measure yourself by whether the customer is better off, and you do what it takes to make sure they are.

 - Fluent in SQL and at least one BI tool in production (Looker, Tableau, Sigma, Metabase), comfortable turning raw, inconsistent data into something trustworthy.

Bonus

 - You've built business intelligence for trades operators, ideally in PE value creation/portfolio ops, or in-house at a multi-site operating business, and you know which numbers run a P&L.

 - Direct experience with ServiceTitan, HouseCallPro, or other CRM data models.
